content.verdict: Blue Waters wraps around two pretty coves on Antigua's quiet northwest coast, with a dedicated adults-only Hideaway section that gives couples the privacy of a smaller resort while still accessing the full Blue Waters facilities. Less expensive than Hermitage Bay or Jumby Bay, it's a strong mid-luxury option for honeymooners who want adults-section privacy without the all-inclusive premium.
content.best_room: The Hideaway Suites are the dedicated adults-only collection — set in a separate enclave at the eastern end of the resort with their own infinity pool, restaurant (The Pavilion), and beach access. Hideaway Beachfront Suites with private plunge pools are the honeymoon sweet spot. The four- and five-bedroom Rock Cottages and Cove Suites offer larger family-style accommodations on the main side.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Hideaway Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from V.C. Bird International (15 minutes — Blue Waters is one of Antigua's closest resorts to the airport). Welcome at the Hideaway side of the resort with rum punch and a tour. Settle into a Hideaway Suite, swim in the adults-only infinity pool, and dinner at The Pavilion with the cove view.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Beach Day & Spa
itinerary_7_nights.description: Blue Waters' two coves are calm and swimmer-friendly. Morning paddleboard and snorkel along the rocky headland separating the bays. Afternoon at the Elemis spa for a couples' treatment, then sunset cocktails at Bartley's beach bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Nelson's Dockyard & English Harbour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive south across the island (45 minutes) to Nelson's Dockyard, the UNESCO World Heritage Georgian naval dockyard. Lunch at one of the harbourside restaurants among the restored 18th-century buildings, and an afternoon walk through the historic site.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Shirley Heights Sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Time the day to reach Shirley Heights, the 18th-century military lookout above English Harbour, for sunset. The panorama down to the yachts in Falmouth Harbour is Antigua's most photographed view. Sunday evenings host the iconic steel band sunset gathering.

itinerary_7_nights.title: 365 Beaches Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Antigua's 365 beaches — one for every day of the year — invite a day of beach-hopping. A private driver day visits Half Moon Bay (Atlantic surf), Pink Sand Beach, Ffryes Bay (beach bars), and Darkwood Beach. Return to Blue Waters for dinner at Bay House.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stingray City & Sailing
itinerary_7_nights.description: Stingray City is just 15 minutes from Blue Waters by boat — a shallow sandbar off the north coast where southern stingrays glide around guests in waist-deep water. Combine with a snorkel stop at one of the offshore reefs. Afternoon at the resort for a Hobie Cat sail.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Cove Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last swim in the Hideaway cove, breakfast on the suite terrace, and a quick 15-minute road transfer back to V.C. Bird International for international connections. Blue Waters' airport proximity makes departure logistics genuinely effortless.

caveats: Blue Waters is not entirely adults-only — only the Hideaway section is dedicated. Couples should book Hideaway specifically to access the adults-only pool, restaurant, and beach.
The main-side family pool and restaurants can be busy during school holidays, though the Hideaway maintains its adults-only character.
The northwest coast location is quieter than the western beach strip but still close to the airport — convenient but less remote than Hermitage Bay or Galley Bay.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Blue Waters is a strong honeymoon choice for couples booking the Hideaway adults-only section — the private infinity pool, dedicated Pavilion restaurant, and beachfront suites with plunge pools deliver a quiet adults experience at lower rates than the island's top properties. Couples on the main side get less of that benefit.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is high season — dry and sunny. February-March is peak. May, June, and November offer better rates. September-October is hurricane season; the resort sometimes reduces operations.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: For Christmas, New Year, and February-March, book 6-9 months ahead. Hideaway Beachfront Suites with plunge pools sell out first. Shoulder season offers good availability with 2-4 months lead time.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Only the Hideaway section is adults-only — the main resort welcomes families. For honeymoons, book Hideaway specifically. Couples wanting fully adults-only properties should consider Hermitage Bay, Galley Bay, Cocobay, or Cocos.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Hideaway Beachfront Suites with private plunge pools — direct access to the adults-only cove, the dedicated Pavilion restaurant, and the infinity pool. Hideaway Garden Suites offer the same access at lower rates.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to V.C. Bird International Airport (ANU). Blue Waters is just 15 minutes by road on the northwest coast — the resort arranges private transfers. Antigua receives direct flights from London, New York, Miami, Atlanta, Toronto, and major European hubs.
